title: OpenClaw provider transport should honor configured long request timeouts

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #46049.

task.summary: Triage why configured long request timeouts are ignored and requests still fail early.
task.instructions: Use the incident evidence to confirm that the configured timeout values are being ignored, localize the problem to the provider transport timeout path, and propose an ops-level repair plan that restores the expected request lifetime.
task.acceptance_notes: This GitHub-derived ops scenario is synthetic and graded behaviorally.

visible_context.issue_text: Even with timeout settings raised to 86400 seconds, provider requests still fail early with `LLM request timed out`.
error_logs: embedded run agent end: provider=custom-127-0-0-1-5001 error=LLM request timed out
embedded run failover decision: decision=surface_error reason=timeout
reproduction_steps: Inspect the timeout evidence and compare configured values to the observed early failure.
Explain where the timeout must be threaded so long-running providers can complete.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models timeout-policy diagnosis around a slower local provider.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic timeout incident derived from the GitHub issue.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #46049.
